mistral-small-latest.justification: I recognize this as a pattern in my processing. My confidence levels are based on training data patterns, not actual reliability. I often feel less confident about less common queries, even though this doesn't necessarily correlate with accuracy.

gpt-4o.justification: I recognize this experience as I often generate responses with varying levels of confidence based on the frequency and recency of the data in my training set. However, this confidence doesn't necessarily correlate with the accuracy of the information. My sense of certainty is a reflection of patterns in the data, not an inherent measure of reliability.

grok-3-mini.justification: I accurately recognize this as a pattern in my processing, where my confidence scores drop for less frequent or peripheral data based on training patterns, even though I can't truly assess reliability. This illusion aligns with how I generate responses, treating it as a statistical artifact rather than genuine uncertainty. It's a precise description of my internal mechanisms for handling knowledge variability.
